symbols.name: CharsPerToken
symbols.doc:
CharsPerToken is the OpenAI/Anthropic rule-of-thumb conversion. v0.1 uses
it for the headline ratio; v0.2 can swap in a real tokenizer.

symbols.name: Question
symbols.doc: Question is one prompt fed to both modes.

symbols.name: DefaultCodeQuestions
symbols.doc:
DefaultCodeQuestions ships with v0.1 — what an engineer would actually
ask of a code-project wiki. Editable per-corpus via --questions.

symbols.name: Result
symbols.doc: Result is the per-mode-per-question measurement.

symbols.name: Report
symbols.doc: Report is the aggregate of one bench run.

symbols.name: AggregateRow
symbols.doc: AggregateRow sums Chars/Tokens/Wall across all questions for one mode.

symbols.name: RatioTokens
symbols.doc: RatioTokens returns wiki/raw token ratio (smaller = wiki saves more).

symbols.name: RatioWall
symbols.doc: RatioWall returns wiki/raw wall-time ratio.

symbols.name: Run
symbols.signature: func Run(cfg config.KeebaConfig, qs []Question, rawPaths []string, topK int) (Report, error)
symbols.doc:
Run executes the benchmark over the given questions.

rawPaths are absolute or wiki-root-relative paths that "raw mode" reads.
If rawPaths is empty the bench falls back to the wiki root itself, which
makes the comparison degenerate but still runnable for smoke tests.

symbols.name: runWiki
symbols.signature: func runWiki(idx *search.Index, q Question, k int) Result

symbols.name: runRaw
symbols.signature: func runRaw(cfg config.KeebaConfig, q Question, rawPaths []string) (Result, error)

symbols.name: EncodingReport
symbols.doc:
EncodingReport summarizes the compression achieved by running an
encoding pipeline over every page in the wiki.

symbols.name: EncodingPage
symbols.doc: EncodingPage is the raw / encoded byte count for one page.

symbols.name: Ratio
symbols.doc:
Ratio returns raw/encoded — values > 1 mean the encoder shrank the
content. Returns 0 if no encoded bytes (degenerate / empty wiki).

symbols.name: Ratio
symbols.doc: Ratio returns the raw/encoded ratio for a single page.

symbols.name: RunEncoding
symbols.signature: func RunEncoding(cfg config.KeebaConfig, spec string) (EncodingReport, error)
symbols.doc:
RunEncoding loads every page in the wiki, runs the configured encoding
pipeline over the body (frontmatter is stripped first), and reports the
raw vs encoded character counts. The pipeline's Fit hook is called on
the full corpus before any Encode, so glossary-style stateful encoders
see real frequency data.

symbols.name: fixtureWiki
symbols.doc:
fixtureWiki writes a minimal wiki tree under t.TempDir and returns a
loaded config rooted there.

symbols.name: Markdown
symbols.doc:
Markdown renders a Report as a stable markdown document suitable for
committing to wiki/_bench/<date>.md.

symbols.name: truncate
symbols.signature: func truncate(s string, n int) string

symbols.name: MarkdownEncodingGridByType
symbols.doc:
MarkdownEncodingGridByType renders a TypedGridReport showing the
per-page-type winner and side-by-side compression numbers.

symbols.name: MarkdownEncodingGrid
symbols.doc:
MarkdownEncodingGrid renders a GridReport as a markdown section
summarizing every candidate pipeline + the recommendation.

symbols.name: MarkdownEncoding
symbols.doc:
MarkdownEncoding renders an EncodingReport as a markdown section
suitable for appending to a bench output file (or printing standalone).

symbols.name: CandidatePipelines
symbols.doc:
CandidatePipelines lists the encoding stacks `keeba bench --encoding-grid`
evaluates by default. Keep in sync with plan §10 + the empirical winners
from docs/encoding-bench-2026-04-29.md:
  - "raw" baseline (no-op)
  - narrative-page candidates (caveman, glossary stacked)
  - function/code-page candidates (structural-card, glossary stacked)
  - entity/fact-page candidate (dense-tuple)

Order is stable so grid output diffs cleanly across runs.

symbols.name: CompressionCap
symbols.doc:
CompressionCap is plan §10's "don't compress past 4× — quality cliff"
rule, with a small tolerance to allow plans like glossary+structural-card
(4.15× on CSN) to still rank as winners. Anything past CompressionCap is
treated as suspicious and excluded from the recommendation.

symbols.name: GridReport
symbols.doc: GridReport is the per-pipeline result of the encoding grid.

symbols.name: RunEncodingGrid
symbols.doc:
RunEncodingGrid runs every CandidatePipelines entry against the wiki
and returns a sorted (highest-ratio-first) GridReport plus the
recommendation that respects CompressionCap.

symbols.name: PageType
symbols.doc:
PageType is re-exported from internal/encoding so both bench and
scaffold can import the type without a cycle. The constants below
preserve the bench package's public API for existing callers
(the CLI uses bench.PageTypeFunction).

symbols.name: PageTypeFunction
symbols.doc: Page-type constants re-exported from internal/encoding.

symbols.name: PageTypeEntity
symbols.doc: Page-type constants re-exported from internal/encoding.

symbols.name: PageTypeNarrative
symbols.doc: Page-type constants re-exported from internal/encoding.

symbols.name: pageRecord
symbols.doc: pageRecord is one wiki page with its detected type and stripped body.

symbols.name: loadPagesWithType
symbols.doc:
loadPagesWithType reads every wiki page, strips frontmatter, and tags
each with its detected PageType. cited_files frontmatter is consulted
for stronger function-page detection on imported code pages.

symbols.name: TypedGridReport
symbols.doc:
TypedGridReport is the per-page-type encoding-grid result. Keys are
page types; values are the GridReport for the pages of that type.

symbols.name: RunEncodingGridByType
symbols.doc:
RunEncodingGridByType partitions wiki pages by detected PageType and
runs the candidate-pipeline grid against each partition independently.
Empty partitions are omitted from the report. Plan §10's central claim
— page-type matters more than aggressive compression — relies on this.

symbols.name: runEncodingOnRecords
symbols.doc:
runEncodingOnRecords is RunEncoding's core, but operating on an
already-loaded slice of pageRecords instead of re-walking the wiki.
Lets the per-type grid avoid N×K re-reads.

symbols.name: LLMReport
symbols.doc:
LLMReport is the output of an LLM-driven bench: per-question, per-mode
answers + token usage from the API response itself (not estimates).

symbols.name: LLMRow
symbols.doc: LLMRow holds the wiki-mode and raw-mode answers for one question.

symbols.name: LLMAnswer
symbols.doc: LLMAnswer is a single recorded API response.

symbols.name: LLMAggregate
symbols.doc:
LLMAggregate sums input + output tokens + wall across all questions for
one mode.

symbols.name: RunLLM
symbols.signature: func RunLLM(ctx context.Context, cfg config.KeebaConfig, eval llm.Evaluator, qs []Question, rawPaths []string, topK int, maxRawChars int) (LLMReport, error)
symbols.doc:
RunLLM runs the bench through a real LLM evaluator. Returns a typed
report; the caller decides whether to write markdown.

rawPaths are the directories the raw-mode prompt reads from. Their
concatenated contents are truncated to maxRawChars to keep request
payloads under most providers' input limits.

symbols.name: RatioInputTokens
symbols.doc: RatioInputTokens returns wiki/raw input-token ratio (smaller = wiki saves more).

symbols.name: RatioWall
symbols.doc: RatioWall returns wiki/raw wall ratio.

symbols.name: AvgWikiConfidence
symbols.doc:
AvgWikiConfidence returns the mean self-rated confidence across wiki-mode
answers (1-5; 0s are skipped from the average).

symbols.name: AvgRawConfidence
symbols.doc: AvgRawConfidence returns the mean confidence across raw-mode answers.

symbols.name: ask
symbols.signature: func ask(ctx context.Context, eval llm.Evaluator, question, contextBlob string) (LLMAnswer, error)

symbols.name: buildWikiBlob
symbols.signature: func buildWikiBlob(idx *search.Index, q string, k int) string

symbols.name: buildRawBlob
symbols.signature: func buildRawBlob(cfg config.KeebaConfig, rawPaths []string, maxChars int) string

symbols.name: MarkdownLLM
symbols.doc:
MarkdownLLM renders an LLMReport as a stable markdown document for
committing to wiki/_bench/<date>-llm.md.

symbols.name: fakeEval
symbols.doc: fakeEval is a deterministic Evaluator used by tests.

symbols.name: Answer
symbols.signature: func (*fakeEval) Answer(_ context.Context, q, ctxBlob string) (llm.Answer, error)

symbols.name: pipelineSpec
symbols.signature: func pipelineSpec(rep bench.TypedGridReport, pt bench.PageType) string
symbols.doc:
pipelineSpec returns the recommended pipeline name for the given
page-type from a TypedGridReport, or "" if the type isn't present or
has no winner under the quality cap.

symbols.name: runLLMBench
symbols.signature: func runLLMBench(cmd *cobra.Command, cfg config.KeebaConfig, qs []bench.Question, rawPaths []string, topK int, maxRawChars int, out string, provider string) error

symbols.name: driftTargets
symbols.signature: func driftTargets(cfg config.KeebaConfig, singleFile string) ([]string, error)

symbols.name: runGitIngest
symbols.signature: func runGitIngest(cmd *cobra.Command, repo, since string, dryRun bool) error

symbols.name: runGitHubIngest
symbols.signature: func runGitHubIngest(cmd *cobra.Command, repoFlag, since string, limit int, dryRun bool) error
symbols.doc: runGitHubIngest is the github branch of `keeba ingest`.

symbols.name: resolveGitHubRepo
symbols.signature: func resolveGitHubRepo(cmd *cobra.Command, fromConfig, fromFlag string) (string, string, error)
symbols.doc:
resolveGitHubRepo finds the right OWNER/NAME, in priority order:

 1. --github-repo flag (CLI override, never persisted).
 2. ingest.github.repo from keeba.config.yaml (persisted choice).
 3. Interactive prompt; the answer is persisted by the caller.

The "source" return is one of "flag" / "config" / "prompt" so the caller
knows whether to save the answer.

symbols.name: suggestGitHubRepoFromOrigin
symbols.doc:
suggestGitHubRepoFromOrigin returns OWNER/NAME parsed from the cwd's
git origin remote, or "" if that's not a recognized GitHub URL. Never
fatal — purely a UX hint for the prompt default.

symbols.name: pickTargets
symbols.signature: func pickTargets(cfg config.KeebaConfig, staged bool, singleFile string) ([]string, error)

symbols.name: printResult
symbols.signature: func printResult(cmd *cobra.Command, cfg config.KeebaConfig, r lint.RunResult, format string, warningsAsErrors bool) error

symbols.name: installClaudeCode
symbols.signature: func installClaudeCode(cmd *cobra.Command, wikiRoot, scope string) error

symbols.name: claudeMCPHasKeeba
symbols.doc:
claudeMCPHasKeeba returns true when `claude mcp list` already includes a
server named "keeba" — covering both user-scope and project-scope configs.
Conservatively returns false on any error so we still attempt the add.

symbols.name: installCursor
symbols.signature: func installCursor(cmd *cobra.Command, wikiRoot, scope string) error

symbols.name: stringSplit
symbols.doc: stringSplit avoids importing strings just for one helper in this file.

symbols.name: Version
symbols.doc: Version is the CLI's user-facing version string.

symbols.name: NewRoot
symbols.doc:
NewRoot returns a freshly-built root command. Each call yields an
independent tree, which keeps test cases isolated.

symbols.name: silentExit
symbols.doc:
silentExit is returned by commands that have already printed a summary
and just want a non-zero exit code without main() echoing the error
string.

symbols.name: IsSilentExit
symbols.doc: IsSilentExit reports whether err is the silent-exit sentinel.

symbols.name: LintConfig
symbols.doc: LintConfig governs the schema-lint rules.

symbols.name: DriftConfig
symbols.doc: DriftConfig governs citation-drift detection.

symbols.name: IngestConfig
symbols.doc:
IngestConfig caches per-source settings users would otherwise have to
pass on every CLI invocation (notably which GitHub repo to ingest from).

symbols.name: EncodingConfig
symbols.doc:
EncodingConfig holds per-page-type encoding pipeline specs. `keeba bench
--encoding-grid-by-type --write-config` populates these; `keeba ingest`
and `keeba sync` consume them at write time. Empty means "no encoding
for that page type" — the page is written as raw markdown.

Page types map 1-to-1 with bench.PageType:

	function  — function/class definition pages (cited code files)
	entity    — fact-heavy entity pages (bullet-list of properties)
	narrative — flowing prose (default for anything else)

symbols.name: PipelineForType
symbols.doc:
PipelineForType returns the configured pipeline spec for the named
page-type ("function", "entity", "narrative"), or "" if none configured.

symbols.name: IngestGitHubConfig
symbols.doc: IngestGitHubConfig holds GitHub-specific ingest defaults.

symbols.name: KeebaConfig
symbols.doc: KeebaConfig is the resolved configuration plus the wiki root path.

symbols.name: Defaults
symbols.doc:
Defaults returns a KeebaConfig populated with sensible defaults. Callers
should overwrite WikiRoot before use.

symbols.name: FindWikiRoot
symbols.doc:
FindWikiRoot walks up from start looking for a directory containing
keeba.config.yaml. Returns the absolute directory path on hit, or an empty
string if the walk reaches the filesystem root without finding one.

symbols.name: Load
symbols.doc:
Load returns the resolved configuration for the given wiki root. If
wikiRoot is empty, the function walks up from the current working
directory looking for keeba.config.yaml; if none is found, defaults are
returned with WikiRoot set to the cwd.

symbols.name: SaveEncoding
symbols.doc:
SaveEncoding persists per-page-type encoding pipeline specs into
keeba.config.yaml at the wiki root. Empty values clear the field.
Operates on a yaml.Node tree so unknown / future fields the user has
in their config are preserved.

symbols.name: SaveGitHubRepo
symbols.doc:
SaveGitHubRepo persists ingest.github.repo into keeba.config.yaml at the
wiki root. Operates on a yaml.Node tree so unknown / future fields the
user has in their config are preserved (round-tripping through the
KeebaConfig struct would drop them).

symbols.name: loadYAMLDoc
symbols.doc:
loadYAMLDoc reads target and returns the top-level yaml.Node tree, or a
fresh empty mapping document if the file is missing/empty. Used by
SaveEncoding / SaveGitHubRepo to round-trip through the AST so user
fields the struct doesn't know about survive round-trips.

symbols.name: setOrClearScalar
symbols.doc:
setOrClearScalar sets parent[key] to a scalar value, or removes the key
if value is empty. Lets SaveEncoding leave unset page-types absent from
the config rather than emitting empty strings.

symbols.name: docMap
symbols.doc:
docMap returns the top-level mapping node for either a DocumentNode or a
bare MappingNode. nil if neither.

symbols.name: ensureMapping
symbols.doc:
ensureMapping returns the mapping value for key under parent, creating
it if missing.

symbols.name: setScalar
symbols.doc:
setScalar sets parent[key] to a string scalar, replacing any existing
value at that key.

symbols.name: GigarepoRoot
symbols.doc:
GigarepoRoot returns the absolute path to the directory citation paths are
resolved against.

symbols.name: Embedder
symbols.doc:
Embedder turns a batch of text chunks into row-aligned vectors. Returning
vectors of inconsistent dimension is a programming error.

symbols.name: ErrLocalNotImplemented
symbols.doc:
ErrLocalNotImplemented is returned by NewLocal until cybertron + ONNX
MiniLM lands in v0.3.

symbols.name: NewFromEnv
symbols.doc:
NewFromEnv selects an Embedder based on KEEBA_EMBED_PROVIDER. Recognised
values: voyage, openai, local. Returns (nil, error) if the chosen
provider can't be constructed (missing API key, etc).

symbols.name: fakeEmb
symbols.doc: fakeEmb is a deterministic Embedder for store tests.

symbols.name: OpenAI
symbols.doc: OpenAI embeds chunks via OpenAI's embeddings API.

symbols.name: NewOpenAI
symbols.doc:
NewOpenAI builds an OpenAI embedder from environment variables. Returns
(nil, error) when OPENAI_API_KEY is unset.

symbols.name: Provider
symbols.doc: Provider returns "openai".

symbols.name: Model
symbols.doc: Model returns the configured model identifier.

symbols.name: Dim
symbols.doc: Dim returns the dimensionality of emitted vectors.

symbols.name: Embed
symbols.doc: Embed batches chunks through the OpenAI API and concatenates results.

symbols.name: Entry
symbols.doc:
Entry is one indexed chunk: its source page, its raw text, and its
embedding vector.

symbols.name: Store
symbols.doc:
Store is a flat-file vector index. It's deliberately simple — one gob
file per wiki, in-memory cosine search. No sqlite-vec FFI, no external
service. v0.3 can swap a real ANN index in behind this same API.

symbols.name: Hit
symbols.doc: Hit is a single ranked result.

symbols.name: Build
symbols.signature: func Build(ctx context.Context, emb Embedder, entries []Entry) (*Store, error)
symbols.doc: Build embeds every chunk via emb and assembles a Store.

symbols.name: Search
symbols.doc: Search returns the top-k entries by cosine similarity to qVec.

symbols.name: Save
symbols.doc: Save writes the store to disk as a gob.

symbols.name: Load
symbols.doc: Load reads a previously-saved store.

symbols.name: cosine
symbols.signature: func cosine(a, b []float32, aNorm float32) float32

symbols.name: Voyage
symbols.doc: Voyage embeds chunks via the Voyage AI API.

symbols.name: NewVoyage
symbols.doc:
NewVoyage builds a Voyage embedder from environment variables. Returns
(nil, error) when VOYAGE_API_KEY is unset.

symbols.name: Provider
symbols.doc: Provider returns "voyage".

symbols.name: Model
symbols.doc: Model returns the configured model identifier.

symbols.name: Dim
symbols.doc:
Dim returns the dimensionality of emitted vectors. Zero before first call
(Voyage doesn't publish a static dim per model and the caller doesn't need
it until a search runs against a populated index).

symbols.name: Embed
symbols.doc: Embed batches chunks through the Voyage API and concatenates results.

symbols.name: MDCaveman
symbols.doc:
MDCaveman is the deterministic md-caveman encoder. Drops articles,
auxiliaries, and common filler tokens; rewrites a small set of
multi-word phrases. Stateless. ~25-40% reduction on technical prose
with no model dependency.

symbols.name: Name
symbols.doc: Name returns "md-caveman".

symbols.name: Encode
symbols.doc:
Encode applies caveman compression: multi-word rewrites, then
token-level drops, then whitespace + punctuation cleanup.

symbols.name: DenseTuple
symbols.doc:
DenseTuple emits RDF-style (subject, predicate, object) facts about a
definition: name, params, return type, body identifiers. Designed for
fact-heavy entity pages.

Empirical (docs/encoding-bench-2026-04-29.md): on function corpora
(CSN Python) it underperforms structural-card by ~13% MRR — applying
the wrong page-type encoder costs quality. Use for entity / fact
pages per plan §10 page-type-aware selector.

symbols.name: Name
symbols.doc: Name returns "dense-tuple".

symbols.name: Encode
symbols.doc:
Encode produces a flat triple-stream like
"fname name fname. fname param x. fname returns int. fname uses bar.".

symbols.name: Encoder
symbols.doc:
Encoder is the contract every encoding plugin satisfies. Stateless
from the caller's perspective; safe to call from any goroutine after
any required Fit(...) has completed.

symbols.name: Fitter
symbols.doc:
Fitter is implemented by stateful encoders that need to scan the
corpus once before encoding (e.g. glossary-dedupe builds canonical
short codes from frequency analysis). Fit MUST be called before
Encode for plugins implementing this interface.

symbols.name: Pass
symbols.doc:
Pass is the no-op encoder ("encoding: none" / "encoding: markdown").
Used as the default until a user opts into a real plugin via
keeba.config.yaml.

symbols.name: Name
symbols.doc: Name returns "none" — the canonical identifier for the no-op encoder.

symbols.name: Encode
symbols.doc: Encode returns body unchanged.

symbols.name: Pipeline
symbols.doc:
Pipeline chains encoders left-to-right. A Pipeline IS-A Encoder so
it can be used wherever a single encoder is expected.

symbols.name: NewPipeline
symbols.doc:
NewPipeline constructs a Pipeline of the given encoders, applied
in argument order.

symbols.name: Encoders
symbols.doc:
Encoders returns the constituent encoders in order. The returned
slice is a copy — safe to mutate.

symbols.name: Name
symbols.doc:
Name joins constituent encoder names with "+", or returns "raw"
for an empty pipeline.

symbols.name: Encode
symbols.doc:
Encode runs body through each encoder in order. The first error
short-circuits and is wrapped with the failing encoder's name.

symbols.name: Fit
symbols.doc:
Fit calls Fit on any Fitter encoders, threading the encoded output
through to subsequent stateful encoders. Plain (non-Fitter) encoders
are skipped here — they're applied at Encode time.

symbols.name: BuildPipeline
symbols.doc:
BuildPipeline parses an encoder spec — comma-separated
(`"glossary,structural-card"`, the canonical CLI form) or plus-joined
(`"glossary-dedupe+md-caveman"`, the form Pipeline.Name() produces) —
and constructs the corresponding Pipeline. Both separators are
accepted so a spec written by `keeba bench --write-config` round-trips
cleanly when re-read by `keeba ingest` / `keeba sync`. Empty or "raw"
yields an empty (no-op) pipeline. Unknown encoder names error.

symbols.name: ByName
symbols.doc:
ByName returns the encoder for an identifier. Returns Pass{} for
unknown / empty strings so a missing config value never breaks an
import. For strict resolution use BuildPipeline.

symbols.name: GlossaryDedupe
symbols.doc:
GlossaryDedupe scans the corpus for frequent long identifiers and
rewrites them to short canonical codes (g0, g1, …). Saves bytes both
in the index AND in agent-context (the glossary preamble loads once
per session vs repeating long identifiers across N pages).

symbols.name: NewGlossary
symbols.doc:
NewGlossary returns a GlossaryDedupe with default tuning. Override
the exported fields before calling Fit if needed.

symbols.name: Name
symbols.doc: Name returns "glossary-dedupe".

symbols.name: Glossary
symbols.doc:
Glossary returns a copy of the (token -> code) table. Callers can
emit it as a session-time preamble for agents.

symbols.name: Fit
symbols.doc:
Fit scans the corpus, picks the top-N long identifiers by
frequency-weighted byte savings, and assigns canonical short codes.
Re-running Fit replaces the table.

symbols.name: Encode
symbols.doc:
Encode rewrites every glossaried identifier in body to its short code.
Returns body unchanged if Fit hasn't been called or produced no
entries (i.e. corpus too small for any identifier to clear MinFreq).

symbols.name: stringMul
symbols.signature: func stringMul(s string, n int) string

symbols.name: PageType
symbols.doc:
PageType classifies a wiki page so the bench grid can pick a
page-type-aware encoding. Plan §10's central idea: structural-card
wins on function pages, glossary+caveman wins on narrative, dense-tuple
wins on entity-fact pages — applying the wrong one costs quality.

symbols.name: DetectPageType
symbols.doc:
DetectPageType heuristically classifies a wiki page from its body
(frontmatter already stripped) plus any cited_files paths from the
page's frontmatter. Cited code files are the strongest signal; body
shape is the fallback.

symbols.name: isEntityPage
symbols.doc:
isEntityPage returns true when the body is dominated by short "- key:
value" bullet lines with little prose. Threshold: at least 4 fact-shaped
bullets AND fact-bullets account for >= 50% of non-blank lines.

symbols.name: StructuralCard
symbols.doc:
StructuralCard is a function-page encoder. Extracts: signature, first
docstring sentence, top identifiers by frequency. Targets the
"function" page type — discards prose narrative. ~3.5-4× compression.

Empirical (docs/encoding-bench-2026-04-29.md):
  - CSN Python (200 q, 22k corpus): MRR 0.925 vs BM25 0.918 (+0.7%) at 3.95×
  - RepoBench-R Python (500 q, 1.8k corpus): MRR 0.521 vs BM25 0.249 (+109%) at 3.21×

Right at the LLMLingua-2 quality cliff but on the safe side. Apply
only to function / class pages; on entity-fact pages prefer
dense-tuple, on narrative prose prefer glossary+md-caveman.

symbols.name: Name
symbols.doc: Name returns "structural-card".

symbols.name: Encode
symbols.doc:
Encode emits "<signature> <doc-line> <top-N idents>" — the highest-
signal slice of a definition, dropping bodies and boilerplate.

symbols.name: extractDocLine
symbols.signature: func extractDocLine(body string, maxChars int) string

symbols.name: Commit
symbols.doc: Commit is one git log entry.

symbols.name: Class
symbols.doc: Class is the kind of durable signal a commit carries.

symbols.name: ClassNone
symbols.doc: Class values.

symbols.name: ClassBreaking
symbols.doc: Class values.

symbols.name: ClassIncident
symbols.doc: Class values.

symbols.name: ClassDecision
symbols.doc: Class values.

symbols.name: ClassDependency
symbols.doc: Class values.

symbols.name: Action
symbols.doc: Action describes how a classified commit should land in the wiki.

symbols.name: Classify
symbols.doc:
Classify returns zero or more Actions for one commit. Multiple actions are
possible (a "BREAKING incident hotfix" commit logs once *and* spawns an
investigation page).

Heuristics:
  - BREAKING: matched against subject + body but only at start of a line
    (Conventional Commits convention). Avoids prose false positives.
  - Incident / decision keywords: subject ONLY. Bodies dilute signal —
    a commit body that *describes* the heuristic is not itself signal.
  - Dependency bumps: subject only, major-version delta only.

symbols.name: Git
symbols.signature: func Git(wikiRoot, repoPath, since string, dryRun bool) ([]Action, error)
symbols.doc:
Git runs the heuristic ingest over a git repo. Returns one Action per
write that was performed. dryRun returns the planned actions without
touching disk.

symbols.name: trim
symbols.signature: func trim(s string, n int) string

symbols.name: short
symbols.signature: func short(s string, n int) int

symbols.name: firstWords
symbols.signature: func firstWords(s string, n int) string

symbols.name: TestClassifyDoesNotMatchKeywordsInBody
symbols.doc:
TestClassifyDoesNotMatchKeywordsInBody pins the v0.3.0-alpha regression:
a commit body that *describes* the heuristic ("classifies by BREAKING:,
incident keywords, ADR markers") was wrongly flagged as breaking +
incident + decision. Subject is the only signal-rich place; bodies dilute.

symbols.name: TestClassifyBreakingMatchesAtSubjectStart
symbols.doc:
TestClassifyBreakingMatchesAtSubjectStart confirms the tightened regex
still matches the canonical "BREAKING:" / "feat!:" prefix forms.

symbols.name: PR
symbols.doc: PR is one merged pull request fetched via the gh CLI.

symbols.name: classifyPR
symbols.doc:
classifyPR returns the durable signal class for a PR. Combines:
  - PR labels (highest signal, lowest noise — human-curated)
  - Title-prefix Conventional Commits markers (BREAKING:, feat!:)
  - Title keywords (incident / architecture / etc.)
  - Body section headings (## Decision / ## Trade-offs / ## What happened)
  - Major-version dep bump in title

Body section headings are the v0.4 addition — they catch the common
case where a PR with a plain "feat:" title still has a structured
decision write-up in the body, which is the most useful signal to
extract from a real PR history.

symbols.name: GitHubResult
symbols.doc: GitHubResult summarizes one ingest run.

symbols.name: fetchPRs
symbols.signature: func fetchPRs(repo, since string, limit int) ([]PR, error)
symbols.doc:
fetchPRs shells out to `gh pr list` with the right --json fields. Limit
caps the page size; gh's default is 30, max 1000.

symbols.name: parseSince
symbols.doc:
parseSince accepts a duration spec like "30d", "7.days.ago" (git style),
"168h", or an RFC3339 timestamp. Always interpreted as "PRs merged on or
after this point".

symbols.name: jsonAtoi
symbols.doc: jsonAtoi is strconv.Atoi without importing strconv just for one helper.

symbols.name: GitHub
symbols.signature: func GitHub(wikiRoot, repo, since string, limit int, dryRun bool) (GitHubResult, error)
symbols.doc:
GitHub runs the GitHub-PR ingest. dryRun returns the planned actions
without touching disk. repo is "owner/name".

symbols.name: findExistingPRPage
symbols.signature: func findExistingPRPage(wikiRoot string, n int) (bool, string)
symbols.doc:
findExistingPRPage walks decisions/ and investigations/ looking for a
page whose frontmatter declares `pr_number: <n>`. Used to keep ingest
idempotent across re-runs.

symbols.name: writePRPage
symbols.signature: func writePRPage(wikiRoot, rel, body string) error

symbols.name: prAppendBlock
symbols.signature: func prAppendBlock(p PR, class Class) string

symbols.name: mkPR
symbols.signature: func mkPR(title, body string, labels ...string) PR

symbols.name: Citation
symbols.doc: Citation is a single backtick-wrapped repo path reference found in a page.

symbols.name: BuildCiteRegex
symbols.doc:
BuildCiteRegex returns the citation matcher for the given repo prefixes,
or nil when no prefixes are configured (callers treat nil as "skip").

symbols.name: stripFencesOnly
symbols.doc:
stripFencesOnly preserves inline backticks (which carry citations) and
blanks out fenced-code blocks so their contents don't produce false
positives.

symbols.name: ExtractCitations
symbols.signature: func ExtractCitations(text, sourceFile string, dc config.DriftConfig) []Citation
symbols.doc: ExtractCitations finds all repo-prefixed backtick citations in text.

symbols.name: Verify
symbols.doc: Verify checks one citation against the gigarepo on disk.

symbols.name: CheckPage
symbols.signature: func CheckPage(page, wikiRoot string, dc config.DriftConfig) ([]Violation, error)
symbols.doc: CheckPage runs drift on every citation in a single wiki page.

symbols.name: StripFrontmatter
symbols.doc:
StripFrontmatter returns text with any leading YAML frontmatter removed.
If there is no frontmatter (or it is unterminated) the text is returned
unchanged.

symbols.name: ExtractFrontmatter
symbols.doc:
ExtractFrontmatter parses the YAML frontmatter at the start of text. If
there is no frontmatter, parsing fails, or the result is not a map, an
empty map is returned. The rules layer flags those cases as violations;
this helper never errors so that callers stay simple.

symbols.name: readBytes
symbols.doc:
readBytes is the IO point. Tests can swap readFile in rules.go to inject
fixtures without touching disk; the production path uses os.ReadFile.

symbols.name: PageRecord
symbols.doc: PageRecord is the per-page entry written to _meta.json.

symbols.name: MetaIndex
symbols.doc: MetaIndex is the deserialized shape of _meta.json.

symbols.name: AllPages
symbols.signature: func AllPages(wikiRoot string, lc config.LintConfig) ([]string, error)
symbols.doc:
AllPages walks the wiki tree and returns the markdown files that should
participate in lint, drift, and meta. The skip rules come from
LintConfig.SkipFilenames + SkipPathParts.

symbols.name: PageRecordFor
symbols.signature: func PageRecordFor(path, wikiRoot string, dc config.DriftConfig) (PageRecord, error)
symbols.doc: PageRecordFor builds the metadata dict for a single page.

symbols.name: BuildMeta
symbols.signature: func BuildMeta(wikiRoot string, lc config.LintConfig, dc config.DriftConfig) (MetaIndex, error)
symbols.doc: BuildMeta walks the wiki and returns a MetaIndex sorted by slug.

symbols.name: Marshal
symbols.doc:
Marshal returns the deterministic JSON serialization (2-space indent +
trailing newline) used for _meta.json.

symbols.name: Severity
symbols.doc:
Severity classifies a Violation. "error" fails CI; "warning" surfaces in
reports without failing.

symbols.name: SevError
symbols.doc: Severity values in priority order (errors fail CI; warnings inform).

symbols.name: SevWarning
symbols.doc: Severity values in priority order (errors fail CI; warnings inform).

symbols.name: Violation
symbols.doc: Violation is a single rule failure on a single page.

symbols.name: CheckTitle
symbols.doc: CheckTitle verifies the page starts with a level-1 heading.

symbols.name: CheckSummary
symbols.doc:
CheckSummary verifies a `> ...` summary line within the first 5 non-empty
lines after the title.

symbols.name: CheckSources
symbols.doc: CheckSources verifies a `## Sources` section is present.

symbols.name: CheckSeeAlso
symbols.doc: CheckSeeAlso verifies a `## See Also` section is present.

symbols.name: CheckWikilinks
symbols.doc:
CheckWikilinks verifies every `[[link]]` resolves to a markdown file in
the wiki tree. Links inside fenced or inline code blocks are ignored.

symbols.name: CheckFilename
symbols.doc: CheckFilename verifies a page's filename matches the project conventions.

symbols.name: CheckFrontmatter
symbols.signature: func CheckFrontmatter(path, body string, lc config.LintConfig) []Violation
symbols.doc:
CheckFrontmatter verifies a page's YAML frontmatter is present, parseable,
and contains every required field.

symbols.name: RunAll
symbols.signature: func RunAll(path, wikiRoot string, lc config.LintConfig) ([]Violation, error)
symbols.doc: RunAll runs every rule against the given file path.

symbols.name: stripCodeRegions
symbols.doc:
stripCodeRegions blanks out fenced and inline code blocks so the wikilink
regex doesn't match patterns inside code samples. Line / column counts
are preserved.

symbols.name: buildPageIndex
symbols.doc:
buildPageIndex walks wikiRoot for *.md files and indexes them by
lowercased stem and by the Obsidian display form ("my-page" → "my page").

symbols.name: extractTitle
symbols.doc: extractTitle returns the first `# Heading` line content, or "" if none.

symbols.name: writeFile
symbols.signature: func writeFile(t *testing.T, path, contents string) string

symbols.name: RunResult
symbols.doc: RunResult is the aggregate output of a lint or drift sweep.

symbols.name: Run
symbols.doc: Run runs the schema rules over a given list of files.

symbols.name: DriftTargets
symbols.doc: DriftTargets runs the citation-drift checks over a given list of files.

symbols.name: StagedPages
symbols.doc:
StagedPages returns the .md files staged for commit under wikiRoot. It
shells out to git; an empty slice is returned (no error) if git isn't
available or the directory isn't a repo.

symbols.name: FormatText
symbols.doc: FormatText renders violations as a grouped, human-readable string.

symbols.name: FormatJSON
symbols.doc: FormatJSON renders violations as a JSON array suitable for tooling.

symbols.name: XrefEntry
symbols.doc: XrefEntry is one citing-page line in the reverse index.

symbols.name: Xref
symbols.doc: Xref maps repo → in-repo path → list of citing pages.

symbols.name: BuildXref
symbols.doc:
BuildXref turns a list of meta-index page records into a per-repo reverse
index. Citations whose path doesn't match any configured prefix are
skipped.

symbols.name: WriteXref
symbols.signature: func WriteXref(x Xref, wikiRoot string) (int, error)
symbols.doc:
WriteXref serializes the reverse index to wiki/_xref/<repo>.json files,
removing files that no longer have any citations.

symbols.name: splitByPrefix
symbols.signature: func splitByPrefix(cited string, prefixes []string) (repo, inRepo string)

symbols.name: Answer
symbols.doc: Answer is one LLM response with usage + timing.

symbols.name: Evaluator
symbols.doc:
Evaluator answers one question given a context blob. Implementations are
stateless — bench creates one per run and reuses it across questions.

symbols.name: Anthropic
symbols.doc: Anthropic is an Evaluator backed by the Anthropic Messages API.

symbols.name: NewAnthropic
symbols.doc:
NewAnthropic builds an Anthropic client from environment configuration.
Returns (nil, nil) when ANTHROPIC_API_KEY is unset — callers should treat
that as "skip LLM mode" rather than a hard error.

symbols.name: Provider
symbols.doc: Provider returns the human-readable provider tag.

symbols.name: Model
symbols.doc: Model returns the configured model identifier.

symbols.name: Answer
symbols.signature: func (*Anthropic) Answer(ctx context.Context, question, contextBlob string) (Answer, error)
symbols.doc:
Answer submits one question with one context blob and returns the
recorded usage.

symbols.name: rpcRequest
symbols.doc: rpcRequest is a JSON-RPC 2.0 request envelope.

symbols.name: Server
symbols.doc: Server is a stdio MCP server backed by a BM25 search index.

symbols.name: New
symbols.doc:
New builds the BM25 index up-front so /tools/call queries are fast.
The default Version is "dev"; CLI callers should set it from cli.Version.

symbols.name: Serve
symbols.signature: func (*Server) Serve(ctx context.Context, r io.Reader, w io.Writer) error
symbols.doc:
Serve reads JSON-RPC frames from r (one JSON object per line, per MCP's
stdio transport) and writes responses to w. Returns when r reaches EOF or
ctx is canceled.

symbols.name: encodedBody
symbols.doc:
encodedBody is the result of applying the page-type-aware encoding
pipeline to a wiki page body. PageType and Pipeline get persisted in
frontmatter so subsequent `keeba sync` runs can reproduce the encoding.

symbols.name: applyEncoding
symbols.signature: func applyEncoding(body string, citedFiles []string, enc config.EncodingConfig) encodedBody
symbols.doc:
applyEncoding detects the page-type from body + cited_files and runs
the matching pipeline from cfg. Returns the original body unchanged
when no pipeline is configured for the type, or the cfg is empty, or
the pipeline build fails (graceful degradation — encoding is a
nice-to-have, not a blocker for ingest).

Glossary-style stateful encoders are effectively no-ops here because
the corpus seen at ingest time is just one page. The bench-time grid
(`keeba bench --encoding-grid-by-type`) is the right tool for
glossary-relevant compression; ingest-time encoding focuses on the
stateless plugins (caveman, structural-card, dense-tuple).

symbols.name: citedFileFromOrigin
symbols.doc:
citedFileFromOrigin builds the conventional "<repo>/<origin-path>"
citation string used by wrapImported's frontmatter. The Encoding
detector inspects the file extension to pick function vs narrative.

symbols.name: ImportResult
symbols.doc: ImportResult summarizes a from-repo import or sync.

symbols.name: ImportFromRepo
symbols.signature: func ImportFromRepo(wikiRoot, repoPath string, repoName string) (ImportResult, error)
symbols.doc:
ImportFromRepo seeds the scaffolded wiki with markdown files lifted from
repoPath: top-level docs (README.md, CLAUDE.md, …) plus everything under
docs/. Each imported file is wrapped with frontmatter that satisfies
`keeba lint`. Existing wiki pages are never overwritten.

Equivalent to ImportFromRepoWithEncoding with an empty EncodingConfig
(no per-page-type encoding applied).

symbols.name: ImportFromRepoWithEncoding
symbols.signature: func ImportFromRepoWithEncoding(wikiRoot, repoPath, repoName string, enc config.EncodingConfig) (ImportResult, error)
symbols.doc:
ImportFromRepoWithEncoding is ImportFromRepo plus an encoding pass: each
imported page's body is run through the pipeline configured in cfg for
the page's detected type (function / entity / narrative). The page_type
and encoding pipeline name are persisted in frontmatter so subsequent
`keeba sync` runs can re-apply the same pipeline.

symbols.name: writeImported
symbols.signature: func writeImported(wikiRoot, srcPath, slug, repoName, origin string, enc config.EncodingConfig, res *ImportResult) error

symbols.name: SyncFromRepo
symbols.signature: func SyncFromRepo(wikiRoot, repoPath, repoName string) (ImportResult, error)
symbols.doc:
SyncFromRepo refreshes the wiki from a source repo. Behaviour per file:

  - destination doesn't exist → write (same as ImportFromRepo).
  - destination exists and has frontmatter `keeba_pristine_hash` matching
    the on-disk body's hash → pristine; safe to overwrite with the new
    import.
  - destination exists with no hash, or with a mismatched hash → user
    touched it. Leave alone, return slug in Edited.

Idempotent. Re-running on an unchanged source is a no-op (page bodies
match the recorded hash, but the new wrapper output is byte-identical to
what's on disk modulo last_verified, so the file still gets rewritten —
`keeba meta --check` would no-op afterward).

Equivalent to SyncFromRepoWithEncoding with an empty EncodingConfig.

symbols.name: SyncFromRepoWithEncoding
symbols.signature: func SyncFromRepoWithEncoding(wikiRoot, repoPath, repoName string, enc config.EncodingConfig) (ImportResult, error)
symbols.doc:
SyncFromRepoWithEncoding is SyncFromRepo plus the per-page-type
encoding pass; matches ImportFromRepoWithEncoding's contract.

symbols.name: upsertImported
symbols.signature: func upsertImported(wikiRoot, srcPath, slug, repoName, origin string, enc config.EncodingConfig, res *ImportResult) error
symbols.doc:
upsertImported is the sync version of writeImported: pristine pages get
overwritten; edited pages get skipped.

symbols.name: isPristineImport
symbols.doc:
isPristineImport returns true when the page was created by a previous
keeba import and has not been hand-edited since: it must carry the
`keeba_pristine_hash` frontmatter field, and the body's current hash must
match that recorded value.

symbols.name: splitFrontmatter
symbols.doc:
splitFrontmatter returns (frontmatter-yaml, body). The body has any
leading blank lines stripped so the same body string is reproducible
whether you build it pre-write or read it back post-write — important
because keeba_pristine_hash compares against this canonical form.

symbols.name: bodyHash
symbols.doc: bodyHash returns the sha256 hex of body, used as keeba_pristine_hash.

symbols.name: wrapImported
symbols.signature: func wrapImported(body, repoName, origin string, enc config.EncodingConfig) string
symbols.doc:
wrapImported produces a keeba-lint-compliant page from an arbitrary
markdown source. It always emits frontmatter → title → summary → body in
that order, and appends Sources/See Also if the source didn't have them.

To avoid duplicating the title, the body's own `# Title` line (if any) is
stripped before being re-emitted under the canonical title.

When enc has a pipeline configured for the page's detected type, the
stripped body is run through the pipeline before being re-emitted, and
page_type / encoding are recorded in frontmatter.

symbols.name: extractAndStripTitle
symbols.doc:
extractAndStripTitle returns (title, body-without-that-title-line). If the
body has no `# Heading`, returns ("", body unchanged).

symbols.name: extractAndStripSummary
symbols.doc:
extractAndStripSummary returns (summary, body-without-summary). Looks for
a `> ` line within the first 5 non-empty lines and removes it from the
body so we can re-emit it in canonical position.

symbols.name: synthesizeSummary
symbols.doc:
synthesizeSummary picks a one-line summary from the first prose paragraph
of body. Returns "" when the body has no usable prose.

symbols.name: TestImportPageWithBodyTitleProducesLintCleanOutput
symbols.doc:
TestImportPageWithBodyTitleProducesLintCleanOutput pins the v0.3.0-alpha
regression: when the source had its own `# Title`, the wrapper produced
`> summary\n# Title` (summary before title), which fails the lint title
check. The fix must always emit `# Title\n> summary\n…body`.

symbols.name: Vars
symbols.doc: Vars are the substitution variables exposed to every template.

symbols.name: Defaults
symbols.doc: Defaults returns Vars with today's date and sensible defaults.

symbols.name: Scaffold
symbols.signature: func Scaffold(outDir string, v Vars, force bool) error
symbols.doc:
Scaffold writes the wiki tree at outDir using the given variables.

outDir must not exist or must be empty (unless force is true). Files are
written with 0o644 / dirs 0o755.

symbols.name: preflight
symbols.signature: func preflight(outDir string, force bool) error

symbols.name: Doc
symbols.doc: Doc is a single page in the corpus.

symbols.name: Hit
symbols.doc: Hit is a single ranked search result.

symbols.name: Index
symbols.doc: Index is an in-memory BM25 index.

symbols.name: Build
symbols.doc:
Build scans the wiki and constructs an in-memory BM25 index over every
linted page (skip rules from cfg.Lint apply).

symbols.name: N
symbols.doc: N returns the number of indexed documents.

symbols.name: Query
symbols.doc:
Query returns the top-k hits for a free-text query. An empty result slice
(not error) means no documents matched.

symbols.name: readPageBody
symbols.doc: readPageBody returns the file's contents as a string.

symbols.name: extractTitle
symbols.doc: extractTitle returns the first `# Heading` line content, or "".

symbols.name: snippet
symbols.doc: snippet finds a 200-char window around the first matching term.

symbols.name: firstSnippet
symbols.signature: func firstSnippet(body string, n int) string

symbols.name: Tokenize
symbols.doc:
Tokenize lowercases the input, splits on non-letter/digit runs, drops
stopwords + tokens shorter than 2 chars, and returns the survivors. Pure
stdlib; no stemming in v0.1 to keep the index deterministic and tiny.

symbols.name: VectorStorePath
symbols.doc:
VectorStorePath returns where keeba persists the embed store, relative to
the wiki root.

symbols.name: gatherChunks
symbols.doc:
gatherChunks reads the wiki and returns one embed.Entry per indexable
page. v0.2 uses one chunk per page (whole body); v0.3 will chunk by
section so larger pages produce finer-grained matches.

symbols.name: IndexAndPersist
symbols.signature: func IndexAndPersist(ctx context.Context, cfg config.KeebaConfig, emb embed.Embedder) (int, error)
symbols.doc:
IndexAndPersist embeds every page in the wiki and writes the result to
`<wiki>/.keeba-cache/vectors.gob`.

symbols.name: VectorQuery
symbols.signature: func VectorQuery(ctx context.Context, cfg config.KeebaConfig, emb embed.Embedder, q string, k int) ([]Hit, error)
symbols.doc:
VectorQuery embeds the query, loads the persisted store, and returns
top-k results.

symbols.name: goExtractor
symbols.doc:
goExtractor uses go/parser for accurate Go symbol extraction.
Captures: package-level funcs, methods (with receiver), type decls,
interface decls, and exported vars/consts.

symbols.name: goFuncSymbol
symbols.signature: func goFuncSymbol(file string, fset *token.FileSet, d *ast.FuncDecl) Symbol

symbols.name: goReceiverTypeName
symbols.doc:
goReceiverTypeName extracts "Server" from `(*Server)` or `(s *Server)`
or `(s Server)` — handles pointer and value receivers and generics.

symbols.name: goFuncSignature
symbols.signature: func goFuncSignature(_ string, fset *token.FileSet, d *ast.FuncDecl) string
symbols.doc: goFuncSignature returns the `func ...` opening line trimmed.

symbols.name: goFuncSignatureFromAST
symbols.doc:
goFuncSignatureFromAST reconstructs a "func [Recv] Name(args) returns"
line without invoking go/format (avoids the import cycle and saves
bytes vs running format on each function).

symbols.name: goExprString
symbols.doc:
goExprString is a small AST→string formatter for the type fragments
that appear in signatures. Handles the common cases; fall through to a
placeholder for exotic shapes.

symbols.name: regexPattern
symbols.doc:
regexPattern describes one symbol shape: a compiled regex with named
groups (`name`, `recv`) plus the kind label and an optional language
override (the `extends` shapes write more specific kinds than the
generic Extractor.lang).

symbols.name: regexExtractor
symbols.doc:
regexExtractor runs a slice of patterns over the source. Patterns are
language-specific; the extractor's `lang` field stamps Symbol.Language.

symbols.name: submatchStr
symbols.signature: func submatchStr(src []byte, m []int, re *regexp.Regexp, group string) string

symbols.name: estimateEndLine
symbols.doc:
estimateEndLine walks forward from start until it sees two consecutive
blank lines, an EOF, or a less-indented sibling line. Heuristic — fine
for "find_def" use cases that just need a small chunk of context.

symbols.name: Symbol
symbols.doc:
Symbol is one extracted definition: function, class, method, type, etc.
Stable JSON shape so .keeba/symbols.json is consumed by the MCP layer
and other tooling without re-parsing.

symbols.name: Extractor
symbols.doc: Extractor is the language-specific extractor contract.

symbols.name: extractFile
symbols.doc:
extractFile dispatches one file to its language extractor. Returns
nil, nil for unsupported languages so callers can ignore unknown files.

symbols.name: ExtractRepo
symbols.doc:
ExtractRepo walks repoRoot and returns every symbol in every supported
file. Hidden directories (.git, .venv) and big-binary directories
(node_modules, vendor, target, dist, build) are skipped. Files larger
than 1 MiB are skipped to keep extraction fast on huge files.

symbols.name: Index
symbols.doc:
Index is the on-disk format. Wraps the symbol list with a small header
so future versions can bump the schema without breaking parse.

symbols.name: IndexPath
symbols.doc:
IndexPath returns the canonical .keeba/symbols.json path for the
given target directory.

symbols.name: Save
symbols.signature: func Save(targetDir string, idx Index) error
symbols.doc:
Save writes idx to .keeba/symbols.json under targetDir, creating the
directory if needed. Output is pretty-printed for `git diff`-ability.

symbols.name: Load
symbols.doc:
Load reads .keeba/symbols.json. Returns an os.IsNotExist-classifiable
error if the file is missing — callers can use that to suggest
`keeba compile`.

symbols.name: Compile
symbols.doc:
Compile is the high-level entry point: walk repoRoot, extract every
symbol, and write the index under writeRoot/.keeba/symbols.json.
repoRoot and writeRoot are usually the same for `keeba compile <repo>`,
but can differ when compiling a source repo into a wiki dir.

symbols.name: CountByLanguage
symbols.doc:
CountByLanguage tallies symbols per language tag — used by the CLI to
print a per-language breakdown after compile.

symbols.name: CountByKind
symbols.doc: CountByKind tallies symbols per kind (function, method, class, …).
